confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_296dd225-411f-462a-b336-079a9ed6db88 read back as received

evidence: Current Windows desktop was Narada index 4; leaked Staccato HWND 7603338 still reported Windows desktop membership index 2.
Komorebi state after returning to Narada showed the Staccato HWND inside DISPLAY2 workspace I and invalid rects such as width=-53 and height=-125.
proposal: Define a Windows Desktop Scoped Komorebi Reconciliation capability: after every Windows virtual desktop transition, read current desktop identity, inspect Komorebi-managed HWNDs, query each HWND desktop membership, remove/float/unmanage off-desktop HWNDs from active tiling, retile current-desktop windows, and assert no off-desktop HWNDs or invalid rectangles remain.

Generalize the doctrine: operator surfaces must not assume their own managed object set is authoritative when the host OS has an independent visibility/membership domain. Such domains should be explicit reconciliation boundaries.
payload.recommendation: Treat this as execution work for the Windows PC Site/template and as product doctrine for Narada operator-surface reconciliation.
payload.summary: A live Windows desktop switch test reproduced Komorebi state pollution across Windows virtual desktops. After switching Narada -> Staccato -> Narada, Komorebi admitted a Staccato Windows Terminal HWND into the Narada workspace even though Windows virtual desktop membership still reported that HWND on Staccato. Komorebi then held invalid rectangles with negative width/height. The principled fix is to make external visibility or membership domains, such as Windows virtual desktop identity, first-class reconciliation boundaries for operator-surface state.
payload.title: Operator surfaces need visibility-domain reconciliation boundaries
